title: [CRITICAL] XSS vulnerability in HTML rendering pipeline
description:
## Problem
The renderTextAsHtml function in utils.js (lines 167-169) allows <a> tags with href attributes through DOMPurify sanitization. This permits javascript: URLs to pass through, enabling XSS attacks via prompt injection in LLM responses.

Additionally, summaryPopup.js (lines 414-415) sets innerHTML directly without sanitization when content starts with <ul>.

## Evidence
utils.js:167-169:
ALLOWED_TAGS includes 'a'
ALLOWED_ATTR includes 'href'

summaryPopup.js:414-415:
if (content.startsWith('<ul>')) { contentDiv.innerHTML = content; }

## Impact
Attacker can inject malicious JavaScript through LLM response manipulation, potentially stealing API keys or session data.

## Fix Required
1. Remove 'a' from ALLOWED_TAGS or add FORBID_ATTR: ['href'] with javascript: protocol
2. Add DOMPurify.sanitize() call in summaryPopup.js before innerHTML assignment
3. Consider using textContent where HTML rendering is not required

title: [CRITICAL] XSS vulnerability in HTML rendering pipeline - javascript: URLs allowed
XSS in Chat Message Rendering via unsanitized innerHTML
description:
The chat.js file renders assistant messages by directly assigning HTML content to innerHTML without sanitization. The renderTextAsHtml() function in utils.js uses marked.parse() but the sanitize option is deprecated and does NOT prevent XSS from LLM responses. An attacker who compromises the OpenRouter API or poisons the LLM response could inject arbitrary JavaScript that executes in the extension's chat page context, granting access to chrome.storage (containing API keys).

**Location:** chat.js:504-563 (renderMessages), utils.js:121-124 (renderTextAsHtml)

**Fix:** Implement DOMPurify sanitization before DOM insertion and add CSP headers to chat.html.
close_reason: Fixed: Implemented DOMPurify sanitization to replace deprecated marked.parse() sanitize option, preventing XSS attacks from malicious LLM responses

title: HTML sanitizer can return unsafe original HTML
description: js/htmlSanitizer.js is used before copying or exporting selected page HTML, but sanitizeHtml returns the original input on sanitizer errors and quickCleanHtml intentionally returns the original HTML unchanged. The sanitizer also performs manual cleanup instead of DOMPurify and does not validate dangerous href/src protocols. Evidence: js/htmlSanitizer.js:122-244 returns htmlString in catch; js/htmlSanitizer.js:247-258 quickCleanHtml returns original HTML; pageInteraction.js:704-717 sends quickCleanHtml fallback content to Joplin; pageInteraction.js:625-644 copies sanitizeHtml output to the clipboard as text/html.

title: Joplin popup injects error messages with innerHTML
description: joplinManager.js renders popup body content by assigning htmlContent directly to bodyDiv.innerHTML. The error path interpolates error.message into that HTML string, so API or background error text can be interpreted as HTML in the content-script UI. Evidence: joplinManager.js:227-232 assigns innerHTML; joplinManager.js:602-605 passes an Error paragraph containing error.message.

title: getSettings exposes decrypted tokens to UI callers
description: js/settingsManager.js decrypts API and NewsBlur tokens and returns them in every getSettings response. That response is requested by content scripts and chat/options UI code, even though most callers need only capability flags and non-sensitive settings. This increases the blast radius of any UI/content-script bug and sends secrets across the extension message boundary unnecessarily. Evidence: js/settingsManager.js:65-82 decrypts tokens; js/settingsManager.js:164-177 includes apiKey and newsblurToken in settings; pageInteraction.js:350 and chat.js:130 request getSettings from UI contexts.

title: Summary API error path leaves requestSummary message unanswered
description: In js/summaryHandler.js, handleRequestSummary() handles responseData.error by sending a summaryResult tab message and then returning without calling sendResponse. The background message listener always returns true, so the original requestSummary caller can be left with an unanswered message channel. Evidence: js/summaryHandler.js:228-240 returns on API error; js/summaryHandler.js:310 sends the normal response only on the success path.

title: Joplin fallback path uses pageURL before declaration
description: In pageInteraction.js, handleJoplinIconClick() has a fallback branch when sanitizeHtml removes all content. That branch calls JoplinManager.fetchAndShowNotebookSelection(joplinToken, fallbackContent, pageURL, true) before pageURL is declared later in the function. Because pageURL is a const declared after the branch, this throws a temporal-dead-zone ReferenceError whenever the fallback path is hit. Evidence: pageInteraction.js:712-716 use pageURL; pageInteraction.js:724 declares const pageURL = window.location.href.

title: Feature: Configurable chat quick buttons via Options with immediate-send
Alt+Click jumps scroll; popup shows raw HTML
description: Regression: (1) Alt+Click selects element but page scroll position jumps to top. (2) Summary popup displays literal HTML (e.g., <ul><li>...) instead of rendering, while Chat view renders correctly. Console sample includes [LLM FloatingIcon] Icon created then jump. Investigate floating icon autofocus and HTML rendering pipeline (DOMPurify load + HTML detection).
close_reason: Fixed: prevent scroll jumps when auto-focusing the floating icon after Alt+Click selection (focus({preventScroll:true})). Render HTML summaries in popup by loading DOMPurify in content scripts and trimming/detecting <ul>/<ol> lists before sanitizing.

title: [HIGH] Insecure token migration - no verification before clearing sync storage
description:
## Problem
The migrateTokensToEncryptedStorage function in background.js (lines 63-94) clears tokens from sync storage immediately after calling chrome.storage.local.set() without verifying:
1. encryptSensitiveData returned valid encrypted data
2. The data was actually persisted to local storage

## Evidence
background.js:72-90:
- Calls encryptSensitiveData but doesn't check return value
- Calls chrome.storage.local.set(migrations) without await verification
- Immediately calls chrome.storage.sync.remove() 

## Impact
If encryption fails silently or storage.local.set fails, user loses their API tokens permanently with no recovery path.

## Fix Required
1. Validate encryptSensitiveData returns non-empty strings
2. After storage.local.set, read back and verify data matches
3. Only then call storage.sync.remove
4. Add try/catch with rollback - keep sync tokens if migration fails
5. Log migration success/failure for debugging
close_reason: Fixed: added encryption validation, read-back verification, and try/catch rollback

title: [HIGH] Missing Content Security Policy in manifest.json
description:
## Problem
The manifest.json lacks an explicit content_security_policy definition. While MV3 provides default CSP for extension pages, the extension processes untrusted LLM responses and web content, requiring stricter controls.

## Evidence
manifest.json has no content_security_policy key (verified lines 1-67).

## Impact
Without explicit CSP:
- Inline scripts could execute if injected
- eval-based attacks possible
- No restriction on connect-src for API calls

## Fix Required
Add to manifest.json:
content_security_policy: {
  extension_pages: "script-src 'self'; object-src 'self'; connect-src https://openrouter.ai https://*.newsblur.com http://localhost:*;"
}

title: [HIGH] Insecure token migration - no verification before clearing sync storage
[HIGH] Missing Content Security Policy in manifest.json
Race condition in AbortController storage for chat requests
description:
The chat abort mechanism stores the AbortController instance in chrome.storage.session. Chrome's storage API uses JSON serialization which strips object methods. The AbortController loses its abort() method when retrieved. The check 'typeof controller.abort === function' fails because the storage retrieval returns a plain object.

**Location:** js/chatHandler.js:62-64 (AbortController storage), js/chatHandler.js:134-182 (abort handling)

**Fix:** Use an in-memory Map for controller storage instead of session storage. Store only the requestId in session storage for abort lookup.
close_reason: Fixed: Replaced chrome.storage.session with in-memory Map for AbortController storage to prevent race condition where JSON serialization strips abort() method

title: No input validation on HTML content processing - DoS vector
description:
The processSelectedElement() function in pageInteraction.js processes user-selected HTML content without validating content size, nesting depth, or structure complexity. No limits on: (1) total content size (could be megabytes), (2) DOM nesting depth (could cause stack overflow), (3) number of elements. The Turndown library is called on potentially massive HTML, blocking the main thread.

**Location:** pageInteraction.js:59-199 (processSelectedElement function)

**Fix:** Add MAX_CONTENT_SIZE (1MB), MAX_NESTING_DEPTH (100), MAX_ELEMENT_COUNT (10000) constants and validate before processing.
close_reason: Fixed: Added input validation with MAX_CONTENT_SIZE (1MB), MAX_NESTING_DEPTH (100), and MAX_ELEMENT_COUNT (10000) limits to prevent DoS attacks

title: Broad <all_urls> host permission violates least privilege
description:
The manifest.json declares <all_urls> in both host_permissions and content_scripts.matches, granting the extension access to read and modify content on every website. This is excessive for an extension that only needs access when the user explicitly activates it via Alt+Click. Combined with the XSS vulnerability, this creates a critical attack chain.

**Location:** manifest.json:12-14 (host_permissions), manifest.json:21-23 (content_scripts.matches)

**Fix:** Remove broad host_permissions and rely on activeTab permission with optional_host_permissions for user-granted access.
close_reason: Fixed - removed <all_urls> from host_permissions, added optional_host_permissions, bumped version to 3.9.5

title: Insecure Storage of API Tokens in chrome.storage.sync
description:
The extension stores OpenRouter API keys, NewsBlur tokens, and Joplin tokens in chrome.storage.sync without encryption. Chrome's sync storage synchronizes data across all devices signed into the same Google account via Google's servers. The keys are accessible to Google and vulnerable to: (1) Google account compromise, (2) Browser forensic analysis, (3) Malicious extensions with storage permissions.

**Location:** options.js:1363-1450 (saveSettings), background.js:74-76 (API key retrieval)

**Fix:** Use chrome.storage.local instead of sync for sensitive tokens. Implement AES-GCM encryption using Web Crypto API before storage.
close_reason: Fixed: Implemented AES-GCM encryption for API tokens using Web Crypto API. Tokens are now stored in chrome.storage.local instead of chrome.storage.sync, preventing synchronization to Google servers. Added migration logic to move existing tokens from sync to encrypted local storage.

title: Installed dependency tree is invalid for baseline-browser-mapping
description: Evidence: npm ls --depth=0 --json exits with ELSPROBLEMS because node_modules has baseline-browser-mapping@2.9.18 while package.json requires ^2.9.19. This makes package health checks fail before test/build gates can be trusted.

title: Chat renderer strips all fenced code blocks from assistant messages
description: Evidence: chat.js line 639 renders assistant strings with renderTextAsHtml(stripCodeFences(msg.content)); stripCodeFences at lines 790-795 replaces every fenced block with its inner text and trims it. This removes the code fence semantics from legitimate assistant answers containing code, changing the content before rendering and export.

title: Summary request error popup passes updatePopupContent arguments in the wrong order
description: Evidence: pageInteraction.js lines 499-505, 516-522, and 536-542 call SummaryPopup.updatePopupContent(errorMsg, null, null, true, hasNewsblurTokenStatus). The signature in summaryPopup.js lines 562-570 is (newContent, originalMarkdownArray, pageURL, pageTitle, errorState, hasNewsblurToken, modelName). These error paths pass true as pageTitle and the NewsBlur boolean as errorState, so the popup can render failed requests as a normal chat-capable state and lose NewsBlur visibility.

title: Max price behavior option is not applied
description: The Options page exposes maxPriceBehavior with Fail and Report / Truncate Output choices, but the summarization flow never reads or applies it. settingsManager.getSettings does not fetch or return maxPriceBehavior, constants.js does not define a shared storage key for it, and pageInteraction.js always reports an error when estimatedCost exceeds maxRequestPrice. Evidence: options.html has maxPriceBehavior radios; options.js:62-64 and options.js:1728-1729 save it; js/settingsManager.js:48-57 omits it from keysToFetch; pageInteraction.js:459-475 always fails on estimatedCost > maxRequestPrice.

title: npm test is a failing placeholder
description: The package test script always fails instead of running a project quality gate. Running npm test -- --runInBand produced Error: no test specified and exited non-zero. Evidence: package.json scripts.test is echo "Error: no test specified" && exit 1; command output on 2026-04-24 showed exit code 1.

title: Chat stop can leave stale placeholder after abort
description: chat.js removes the active stream placeholder only in the llmChatStream callback. handleStopRequest sets streaming false and sends abortChatRequest, but it does not remove the streamContainer/modelLabelDiv created for the active request. If the abort path does not produce the original llmChatStream callback promptly, the placeholder remains visible. Evidence: chat.js:433-440 appends model label and streamContainer; chat.js:457-461 removes them only in the llmChatStream response callback; chat.js:738-763 handles Stop without removing the active placeholder.

title: ErrorHandler showToUser path cannot call showError
description: ErrorHandler.handle(error, context, severity, showToUser) tries to notify the user only when typeof showError !== 'undefined'. js/errorHandler.js is an ES module and does not import showError, so showToUser calls from pageInteraction/background modules do not show UI errors through this path. Evidence: js/errorHandler.js:45-47 references showError without importing it; pageInteraction.js:353, pageInteraction.js:427, pageInteraction.js:497, and other call sites pass showToUser=true expecting user-visible reporting.

title: Options model autocomplete outside click can throw
description: options.js shares one autocomplete outside-click handler for language and model inputs. handleGlobalClick() always calls activeAutocompleteInput.closest('.language-input-wrapper').contains(event.target). Model inputs are not inside .language-input-wrapper, so closest() returns null and .contains throws when a model autocomplete is active and the user clicks outside. Evidence: options.js:383-390 attaches document click handler; options.js:518-523 dereferences closest('.language-input-wrapper') without a null check; model inputs are created at options.js:570-581.

title: No-selection error popup omits required onOptions callback
description: summaryPopup.showPopup() rejects calls unless callbacks include onCopy, onChat, onClose, onOptions, and onNewsblur. The no-selection error path in pageInteraction.js calls SummaryPopup.showPopup() without onOptions, so showPopup logs a failure and does not render the intended error popup. Evidence: summaryPopup.js:362-364 validates required callbacks; pageInteraction.js:885-892 passes callbacks without onOptions.

title: Chat UI quick button rendering + immediate-send + disable while streaming
Storage/constants/settings wiring for chat quick buttons
Options UI + persistence for quick button title/prompt rows
QA/build/version/changelog updates for quick buttons
Handle missing Joplin icon when Web Crypto subtle unavailable
close_reason: Content script no longer decrypts local Joplin token; added background token resolver so missing Web Crypto context doesn’t hide icon.

title: Handle undefined Web Crypto subtle in encryption helper
description: Page-script decryption can fail with 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading "importKey")' in production; add stricter Web Crypto capability checks and clearer errors before importKey/generate/decrypt calls.
close_reason: Added defensive Web Crypto capability checks and malformed payload validation; rebuild completed and no remaining undefined-subtle call paths found.

title: Harden encryption helper for Web Crypto context
description: Update encryption utilities to gracefully handle browser variants (crypto/msCrypto and webkitSubtle), avoid direct global crypto usage, and add basic encrypted payload validation before decrypting.
close_reason: Implemented Web Crypto compatibility helper in js/encryption.js, added payload sanity check before decrypt, updated manifest/changelog for 3.9.39, and verified webpack build.

title: in the icon popup the joplin icon is gone
close_reason: Fixed icon visibility by reading encrypted Joplin token from chrome.storage.local when sync token is missing, then rebuilding bundle and bumping version.

title: Silence unload permissions policy violation
description: Chrome console reports: 'Permissions policy violation: unload is not allowed in this document.' This comes from pageInteraction registering window.addEventListener('unload', ...) to run cleanupHighlighter/FloatingIcon.cleanup/SummaryPopup.cleanup. Replace unload listener with pagehide/pageshow (bfcache-safe) or gate behind permissionsPolicy to avoid the violation and console noise.
close_reason: Replaced pageInteraction.js window unload cleanup handler (blocked by Permissions Policy on some documents) with pagehide + pageshow persisted re-init. Rebuilt dist bundle and bumped manifest/changelog.

title: leftover popup after canlelled selection
description:
new problem: alt-click selects a dom element and shows 3-button popup. then click outside the selected dom element, the element is unselected by the popup is not destroyed

full console:


pageInteraction.bundle.js:2 [LLM Utils] Loaded
pageInteraction.bundle.js:2 [LLM Highlighter] Script Loaded (v3.0.3)
pageInteraction.bundle.js:2 [LLM FloatingIcon] Script Loaded (v2.41.0 - ShadowDOM)
pageInteraction.bundle.js:2 [LLM Constants] Loaded
pageInteraction.bundle.js:2 [LLM JoplinManager] Script Loaded
pageInteraction.bundle.js:2 [LLM Content] Script Start (v3.0.25 - Enhanced DOM Cleanup)
pageInteraction.bundle.js:2 [LLM Content] Initial Debug mode: true Joplin Token Loaded: No
pageInteraction.bundle.js:2 [LLM Content] Checking for global 'marked' (should be pre-loaded via manifest).
pageInteraction.bundle.js:2 [LLM Content] Global 'marked' is available.
pageInteraction.bundle.js:2 [LLM Content] Statically imported modules appear to be available.
pageInteraction.bundle.js:2 [LLM Highlighter] Initialized.
pageInteraction.bundle.js:2 [LLM FloatingIcon] Initialized.
pageInteraction.bundle.js:2 [LLM JoplinManager] Initialized.
pageInteraction.bundle.js:2 [LLM Content] Modules initialized flag set to true.
pageInteraction.bundle.js:2 [LLM Content] Script Initialized. Modules ready.
pageInteraction.bundle.js:2 [LLM Highlighter] Alt key down.
pageInteraction.bundle.js:2 [LLM JoplinManager] Loaded last used notebook: web ID: 2e68c3b430e0444986f63c9bf34600d5
pageInteraction.bundle.js:2 [LLM Highlighter] Selecting element: <div class=​Box-sc-62in7e-0
close_reason: Fixed: when selection is cancelled (click outside), handleElementDeselected now also removes the floating icon (not just the summary popup) and resets state.

title: Options: max price debounce + trim prompt user_formatting
description: 1) Options > Models max price input: debounce/timeout while typing is too short; avoid snapping/resetting mid-entry. 2) Options > Advanced > User Formatting Instructions textarea accumulates leading/trailing blank lines (\n\n) over time; normalize on load and on save so stored template is clean.
close_reason: Options: increased max price input debounce (and avoid clamping mid-entry) so typing doesn't snap/reset; normalized prompt <user_formatting> editable section to strip leading/trailing blank lines on load/save.

title: Auto-refresh pricing cache when model IDs change
description: Options UX: when user edits model IDs, automatically refresh knownModelsAndPrices (pricing cache) when a base model ID is not present, and update the Max KiB estimate accordingly. Also raise MAX_MODELS to 10 so defaults don't consume the full quota.
close_reason: Raised Options MAX_MODELS to 10. Added model ID blur handler to auto-refresh knownModelsAndPrices when a new base model ID is not present (no settings auto-save), and recalculates Max KiB after refresh.

title: Compact Options > Models > Model Selection
description: UX: Model Selection rows/cards are too tall/sparse on Options page. Make the model list more compact (reduced padding/gaps, smaller radio cluster) while keeping readability and accessibility.
close_reason: Updated options.css to make Model Selection denser: removed per-row bottom margins, reduced gaps/padding, tightened radio cluster, and slightly reduced remove button size.

title: [MEDIUM] Insufficient URL validation in Joplin API calls
description:
## Problem
The fetchJoplinFoldersAPI function in background.js (line 501) constructs URLs via string concatenation without URL encoding or token format validation.

## Evidence
background.js:501:
const apiUrl = "${JOPLIN_API_BASE_URL}${JOPLIN_API_FOLDERS_ENDPOINT}?token=${joplinToken}";

## Impact
- Malformed tokens could break URL parsing
- Special characters in token not encoded
- No validation that token matches expected format

## Fix Required
1. Validate token format with regex (alphanumeric, 10-100 chars)
2. Use URL API for safe construction:
   const apiUrl = new URL(JOPLIN_API_FOLDERS_ENDPOINT, JOPLIN_API_BASE_URL);
   apiUrl.searchParams.append('token', joplinToken);
3. Apply same pattern to createJoplinNoteAPI
close_reason: Fixed: Added isValidJoplinToken() for regex validation (alphanumeric, 10-100 chars), replaced string concatenation with URL API for safe URL construction in both fetchJoplinFoldersAPI and createJoplinNoteAPI

title: [MEDIUM] Memory leak in activeControllers Map - no size limit
description:
## Problem
The activeControllers Map in js/chatHandler.js (line 10) stores AbortControllers with 60-second timeout cleanup, but has no maximum size limit. Rapid requests before timeouts fire can grow the map unbounded.

## Evidence
js/chatHandler.js:10:
const activeControllers = new Map();

js/chatHandler.js:76-89:
- Adds entry on each request
- 60-second setTimeout for cleanup
- No size check before adding

## Impact
In pathological cases (many rapid requests, or if setTimeout callbacks fail), memory grows unbounded. Service worker could become slow or crash.

## Fix Required
1. Add MAX_CONTROLLERS constant (e.g., 100)
2. Before adding new entry, check size
3. If at limit, abort and remove oldest entries (20%)
4. Add periodic cleanup interval as backup (every 30s)
5. Wrap abort() in try/catch to prevent cleanup failures
close_reason: Fixed: Added MAX_ACTIVE_CONTROLLERS limit (100), eviction of oldest 20% when at capacity, periodic cleanup interval (30s), and wrapped abort() in try/catch

title: [MEDIUM] Decryption failures indistinguishable from missing data
close_reason: Fixed: decryptSensitiveData now returns { success, data, error } result object

title: [MEDIUM] Insufficient URL validation in Joplin API calls
[MEDIUM] Decryption failures indistinguishable from missing data
[MEDIUM] Memory leak potential in activeControllers Map - no size limit
XPath partial class matching in HTML sanitizer removes legitimate content
description:
The htmlSanitizer.js module uses XPath contains(@class, 'ad') which matches partial class names (e.g., 'ad' matches 'ad-banner', 'load-ad', 'loading', etc.). This overly aggressive matching removes legitimate content.

**Location:** js/htmlSanitizer.js:132-148

**Fix:** Replace XPath with CSS selectors using word-boundary matching [class~='classname']. This ensures exact word match, not partial string match.

title: Missing language detection response validation
description:
The detectLanguage() function in summaryHandler.js accepts the LLM's response as a 3-character ISO 639-2 code with minimal validation. Only checks length === 3, not character set (could contain special characters, numbers). No whitelist of valid language codes. Malformed response could cause injection or malformed prompts.

**Location:** js/summaryHandler.js:64-79 (language code validation)

**Fix:** Add regex validation /^[a-z]{3}$/ and whitelist of valid ISO 639-2 codes in constants.js (VALID_LANGUAGE_CODES Set).
close_reason: Fixed: Added language code validation with ISO 639-2 whitelist to prevent injection from malformed LLM responses

title: Inconsistent message handler return patterns cause channel closure
description:
The handleMessage() function in pageInteraction.js has inconsistent return patterns. Some paths return true (async response), others return false or nothing. When chrome.runtime.onMessage.addListener returns false/undefined, Chrome immediately closes the message port. Later sendResponse() calls fail with 'disconnected port object' error.

**Location:** pageInteraction.js:765-809

**Fix:** Always return true from message handlers. Wrap all handling in async IIFE and always call sendResponse() in all code paths.
close_reason: Fixed: handleMessage now always returns true, uses async IIFE, and always calls sendResponse() in all code paths

title: Memory leak from uncleaned event listeners in highlighter.js
description:
The highlighter.js module attaches event listeners to window and document in initializeHighlighter() but does not provide a cleanup function to remove them when the content script is unloaded or reloaded. Old event listeners hold references to DOM elements and prevent garbage collection.

**Location:** highlighter.js:254-265 (initializeHighlighter)

**Fix:** Implement addTrackedEventListener() helper that stores listeners in an array. Export cleanupHighlighter() function that removes all tracked listeners. Call cleanup on window unload event.
close_reason: Fixed memory leak by implementing tracked event listeners and cleanupHighlighter() function

title: Inconsistent error handling patterns cause silent failures
description:
The codebase exhibits inconsistent error handling patterns. Some functions use try-catch, others use callback error parameters, many use a mix. Errors are sometimes logged to console, sometimes shown via showError(), sometimes silently swallowed. The chrome.runtime.lastError pattern is inconsistently applied.

**Location:** pageInteraction.js:247-270, background.js:321-339, js/summaryHandler.js:145-161

**Fix:** Create a centralized ErrorHandler class with handle(), getUserFriendlyMessage(), and trackError() methods. Standardize across all modules.
close_reason: Fixed: Created centralized ErrorHandler class with handle(), getUserFriendlyMessage(), and trackError() methods. Standardized error handling across pageInteraction.js, background.js, and js/summaryHandler.js. Added handleLastError() helper for chrome.runtime.lastError pattern.

title: Release version metadata is inconsistent across manifest, package, changelog, and startup logs
description: Evidence: manifest.json reports 3.9.42, package.json reports 3.9.1, CHANGELOG.md only shows entries through 3.5 in the searched version headings, and startup log strings in background.js/chat.js/options.js/pageInteraction.js/highlighter.js report older independent versions. This breaks release traceability and makes extension builds hard to identify.

title: Default language lists conflict between constants and options UI
description: Evidence: constants.js lines 226-231 exports DEFAULT_PREPOPULATE_LANGUAGES as English, Spanish, Hebrew, French. options.js hardcodes English, Spanish, Hebrew, Mandarin Chinese in at least three default paths: lines 912-917, 1480-1485, and 1830-1835. Reset/load/remove-empty flows therefore do not honor the centralized default.

title: [LOW] Error handler trackError silently ignores chrome.runtime.lastError
close_reason: Fixed: added chrome.runtime.lastError checks to trackError storage callbacks

title: [LOW] Replace magic numbers with named constants
close_reason: Implemented: added SNIPPET_TRUNCATION_LIMIT, MAX_ERROR_LOG_ENTRIES, and notification timeout constants to constants.js; updated all files to use imports

title: [LOW] Remove unused handleLastError import from background.js
description:
## Problem
The audit claims background.js imports handleLastError from errorHandler.js but never uses it. This is dead code that should be removed.

## Evidence
Audit claims location: background.js lines 27-30

## Action Required
1. Verify the import exists and is unused
2. Remove the unused import
3. Check for other unused imports in the file
close_reason: Invalid - handleLastError IS used at line 228 in background.js. The audit claim was incorrect.

title: [LOW] Replace magic numbers with named constants
[LOW] Remove unused handleLastError import from background.js
Remove unused numToWord object (dead code)
description:
The pageInteraction.js file defines a numToWord object at lines 37-44 that is never used anywhere in the codebase. This is dead code that should be removed.

**Location:** pageInteraction.js:37-44

**Fix:** Delete the unused numToWord const declaration.

title: Hardcoded language detection model with no fallback
description:
The detectLanguage() function uses a hardcoded model ID 'moonshotai/kimi-k2' without fallback options. If this model becomes unavailable, deprecated, or the user doesn't have access to it, language detection fails silently and defaults to English.

**Location:** js/summaryHandler.js:21

**Fix:** Add LANGUAGE_DETECTION_MODELS array in constants.js with multiple models. Iterate through models with try-catch fallback on failure.
close_reason: Fixed: Added LANGUAGE_DETECTION_MODELS array with 3 fallback models, updated detectLanguage() to iterate through models with try-catch, logs which model succeeded, and falls back to 'eng' if all fail

title: Missing accessibility attributes on dynamic elements
description:
Several dynamically created elements in options.js and chat.js lack proper accessibility attributes. Autocomplete dropdown items, language flag buttons, and model selection elements should include aria-label, role, tabindex, and keyboard activation support per CONVENTIONS.md.

**Location:** options.js:304-368 (autocomplete dropdown creation)

**Fix:** Add role='listbox' to dropdown container, role='option' and tabindex='0' to items, aria-label attributes, and keydown handlers for Enter/Space activation.

title: Remove LLM-addressed comment artifact in constants.js
description:
The constants.js file contains a comment block at lines 3-9 addressed to 'LLMs' that appears to be a prompt engineering artifact rather than documentation. This is confusing to human developers.

**Location:** constants.js:3-9

**Fix:** Remove the inappropriate comment and replace with standard JSDoc documentation for the constants file.
close_reason: Fixed: Removed LLM-addressed comment artifact and replaced with standard JSDoc documentation in constants.js

title: Use const instead of let for constants in background.js
description:
The background.js file uses 'let' for constants like DEFAULT_BULLET_COUNT and DEFAULT_DEBUG_MODE. These values should never be reassigned and should use 'const' per CONVENTIONS.md.

**Location:** background.js:47-48

**Fix:** Change 'let DEFAULT_BULLET_COUNT = "5"' to 'const DEFAULT_BULLET_COUNT = "5"' and similar for DEFAULT_DEBUG_MODE.
close_reason: Fixed - constants already using const, updated version and changelog

title: Debug logging may leak sensitive data
description:
When DEBUG mode is enabled, the code logs various objects that may contain sensitive information. API key masking is inconsistent and doesn't cover all sensitive fields. Stack traces and error objects may contain tokens.

**Location:** options.js:274-281, utils.js:46-96

**Fix:** Create redactSensitiveData() function that recursively masks keys containing 'apiKey', 'token', 'password', 'secret', 'auth'. Use in all DEBUG logging.
close_reason: Fixed: Added redactSensitiveData() function to utils.js and applied it to DEBUG logging in options.js to prevent leaking sensitive information like API keys and tokens

title: [LOW] Joplin popup accessibility (ARIA + focus)
description:
## Problem
The Joplin notebook selection popup in `joplinManager.js` is dynamically injected but lacks dialog semantics and focus management. This makes the modal difficult to use with screen readers and keyboard-only navigation.

## Current State / Evidence
- The original report mentioned `floatingIcon.js`, but that portion is already addressed: the floating icon container and sub-actions include `aria-label`, `role="button"`, `tabindex`, and keyboard handlers.
- `joplinManager.js` popup HTML does not define dialog semantics:
  - missing `role="dialog"`
  - missing `aria-modal="true"`
  - missing `aria-labelledby` pointing at the popup header
- The notebook autocomplete dropdown uses plain `div` items without listbox/option roles or an announced highlighted/selected state (`aria-selected`).

## Impact
- Violates the accessibility expectations in `CONVENTIONS.md`.
- Screen reader users do not get correct "modal dialog" cues.
- Keyboard focus can escape into the page behind the popup while the popup is visible.

## Fix Required
1. Add dialog ARIA semantics to the Joplin popup root and assign a stable ID to the header for `aria-labelledby`.
2. Add focus management:
   - trap focus within the popup while visible
   - restore focus to the invoking element when the popup closes
   - ensure Escape closes the popup from anywhere inside it
3. Make the autocomplete discoverable to assistive tech:
   - dropdown uses `role="listbox"`
   - items use `role="option"`
   - highlighted item updates `aria-selected` (and/or input uses `aria-activedescendant`)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Popup root has `role="dialog"`, `aria-modal="true"`, and `aria-labelledby` referencing the visible header.
- While the popup is open, Tab/Shift+Tab do not move focus outside the popup.
- Closing the popup restores focus to the element that opened it.
- Pressing Escape closes the popup reliably.
- Autocomplete exposes listbox/option semantics and communicates highlighted state to screen readers.
close_reason: Implemented Joplin popup ARIA + focus trap; verified via webpack build

title: [LOW] Inconsistent error logging levels across codebase
description:
## Problem
The codebase uses console.error, console.warn, and console.log inconsistently. Some error conditions are logged as warnings or info, while some non-errors are logged as errors.

## Evidence
Multiple files use different patterns:
- Some errors logged with console.log
- Some info messages logged with console.error
- No consistent prefix convention

## Impact
- Debugging difficult - hard to filter real errors
- Console noise masks real issues
- No way to programmatically distinguish log levels

## Fix Required
Consider implementing a Logger utility:
const Logger = {
  debug: (msg, ...args) => DEBUG && console.log('[Debug]', msg, ...args),
  info: (msg, ...args) => console.log('[Info]', msg, ...args),
  warn: (msg, ...args) => console.warn('[Warn]', msg, ...args),
  error: (msg, ...args) => console.error('[Error]', msg, ...args)
};

Apply consistently across all files.
close_reason: Implemented centralized Logger utility in js/logger.js with debug/info/warn/error methods. Updated 11 files to use consistent logging levels. Fixed duplicate function in pricingService.js.
